entries.definition:
كتاب الرّاء

رغن

(رغب) الراء والغين والباء أصلان: أحدهما طلبٌ لشيء والآخر سَعَةٌ في شيء.

فالأوَّل الرَّغْبة في الشيء: *الإرادةُ لـه. رغِبْتُ في الشيء. فإِذا لم تُرِدْه قلتَ رغِبتُ عنه. ويقال من الرّغبة: رَغِب يرغَبُ رَغْباً ورُغبا ورَغْبَةً ورَغْبَى مثل شكوى.

disobey’, and ‘Listen’ [adding the insult] ‘may you not hear’, and [they say] ‘rāʿinā’ (originally meaning ‘look after us’, or ‘our Shepherd’), twisting it abusively with their tongues [so as to mean: ‘you foolish one!’ or ‘you evil one’] so as to disparage the religion.
رَاعُونَ rāʿūn [pl. of act. part. راعٍ rāʿī] shepherd; one who observes, fulfils, honours (23:8) وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ لِأَمَٰنَٰتِهِمْ وَعَهْدِهِمْ رَٰعُونَ and those who are faithful to their trusts and pledges.

رِعَايَة riʿāyatun [v. n.; n.] caring, observing, honouring, fulfilling (57:27) وَرَهْبَانِيَّةً ٱبْتَدَعُوهَا مَا كَتَبْنَٰهَا عَلَيْهِمْ إِلَّا ٱبْتِغَآءَ رِضْوَٰنِ ٱللَّهِ فَمَا رَعَوْهَا حَقَّ رِعَايَتِهَا and monasticism they have invented—We did not ordain it for them, only seeking God’s pleasure—but they did not observe it with the observance due to it.

رِعَاء riʿāʾ [pl. of n. rāʿī] specifically signifying herds or flocks of sheep; shepherds, those grazing animals on open land (28:23) قَالَتَا لَا نَسْقِى حَتَّىٰ يُصْدِرَ ٱلرِّعَاء they said, ‘We do not water [our flock] until the shepherds drive [their flocks] away.’

مَرْعَى marʿā [pass. part.; n.; n. of place] pasture; pasturage; place of pasture (87:4) وَٱلَّذِىٓ أَخْرَجَ ٱلْمَرْعَىٰ and who brought out the green pasture.

entries.definition:
r–gh–b desire, wish, to ask for something, to covet, a request; gluttony, a heavy load; a soft load. Of this root, four forms occur eight times in the Qur’an: يَرْغَب yarghab four times; رَغَب raghab once; رَاغِب rāghib once and رَاغِبُونَ rāghibūn twice.
يَرْغَب yarghab [imperf. of v. رَغِبَ raghiba] I [intrans.] 1 [with prep. إِلَى] to ask humbly, to implore, to petition (94:8) وَإِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَٱرْغَبْ and turn humbly to your Lord with your requests 2 [with prep. عَنْ] to reject, to disdain, to shun (2:130) وَمَن يَرْغَبُ عَن مِّلَّةِ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ إِلَّا مَن سَفِهَ نَفْسَهُ who but someone fooling himself, would shun the religion of Abraham? 3 [with prep. بِ] to consider oneself too good for (9:120) وَلَا يَرْغَبُوا۟ بِأَنفُسِهِمْ عَن نَّفْسِهِ they should not consider themselves too good for him, or, they should not put themselves before or above him II [trans.] to wish, to desire (4:127) وَتَرْغَبُونَ أَن تَنكِحُوهُنَّ and you wish to marry them.

373

373
